title: Library Contract Request: Watermark (University of Texas Arlington)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Library Contract Request: Watermark (University of Texas Arlington)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The most recent contract, governing, base, or master license agreement with Watermark Insights, LLC relating to the licensing of Watermark;
All related terms and conditions, terms of service, amendments, addenda, appendices, attachments, product terms, MOUs, MOAs, data processing agreements, and/or letters of intent; 
All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to the contractual relations with Watermark Insights, LLC for faculty information system technology; 
and 
Assessment documentation including, but not limited to, the Higher Education Community Vendor Assessment Toolkit (HECVAT). 

Responsive materials include all contracts, agreements, amendments, appendices, invoices, and any other documents listed above that incorporate language governing terms of use of Watermark.

The scope of this request should encompass more than just direct contracts or renewals between the university and Watermark Insights, LLC; please incorporate all applicable agreements and supporting records facilitated through consortium entities like Lyrasis.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Orlando D-FOIA

title: Flock Safety and ALPR records affecting Grayson

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please route this request to the City Clerk or other designated Open Records Act officer for the City of Grayson.

This is a limited Georgia Open Records Act request for existing records concerning Flock Safety, Flock Group, automated license plate readers, ALPR systems, vehicle recognition cameras, or related camera-sharing systems affecting the City of Grayson, Georgia.

For the period January 1, 2023 through the date this request is processed, please provide:

1. Any contracts, purchase orders, invoices, quotes, proposals, statements of work, grants, memoranda of understanding, data-sharing agreements, intergovernmental agreements, letters of intent, renewal documents, or other agreement documents between the City of Grayson and Flock Safety, Flock Group, Gwinnett County, Gwinnett County Police Department, Gwinnett County Sheriff, or any other party concerning Flock Safety, ALPR cameras, vehicle recognition cameras, or camera-sharing systems.

2. Records sufficient to show whether any Flock Safety, ALPR, or vehicle recognition cameras have been installed, approved, proposed, funded, permitted, discussed, or located within or near the City of Grayson. This includes any existing list, map, agenda packet, staff report, permit record, site plan, right-of-way approval, pole-use approval, or similar record showing camera locations, proposed locations, owner/operator, funding source, or city involvement.

3. Any City Council meeting agendas, minutes, agenda packets, staff reports, presentations, ordinances, resolutions, public safety plans, or legal opinions from January 1, 2023 to the present that mention Flock Safety, Flock Group, ALPR, automated license plate readers, vehicle recognition cameras, camera sharing, surveillance cameras, or public safety cameras.

4. Any current City of Grayson policy, ordinance, resolution, agreement, or written procedure governing approval, permitting, funding, access, retention, oversight, or use of Flock Safety cameras, ALPR cameras, vehicle recognition cameras, or camera-sharing systems.

5. If the City maintains an easily searchable email system, please provide only emails from January 1, 2024 to the present between the City Administrator, City Clerk, Mayor, City Council members, or City Attorney and representatives of Flock Safety or Flock Group that contain any of the following terms: “Flock,” “ALPR,” “license plate reader,” “vehicle recognition,” or “camera sharing.” Please do not begin a broad email search if it will cause the estimated cost to exceed $25 without first providing an itemized estimate.

Please provide records in their original electronic format where available, including PDF, CSV, XLSX, native email export, or other machine-readable format.

If any portion of a record is withheld, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and cite the specific Georgia Code section, subsection, and paragraph relied upon for each withholding or redaction.

Please notify me before incurring costs over $25. If the agency estimates that the cost will exceed $25, please provide an itemized estimate and process items 1 through 4 first before performing any email search.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Don Page

title: Flock Safety audits, network sharing, and Grayson area cameras

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Flock Safety audits, network sharing, and Grayson area cameras
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to this agency’s use of, access to, or data-sharing through Flock Safety, Flock Group, automated license plate readers, ALPR systems, vehicle recognition cameras, and related camera-sharing networks.

For the period January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed, please provide:

1. Organization Audit in Flock. The report should include searches or lookups performed by this agency’s users, including all available fields such as date, time, user, reason/purpose, search type, query type, case number or justification field, and whether the search was manual, alert based, hotlist based, or investigative.

2. Network Audit in Flock. The report should include searches, lookups, hits, alerts, or access to this agency’s Flock network by any other agency, organization, jurisdiction, user group, or external partner.

3. Current Flock network sharing settings, including:
   a. Flock networks shared with this agency;
   b. Flock networks this agency shares with others;
   c. devices, camera groups, or locations included in each share;
   d. permissions granted, including search, alert, hotlist, live-view, investigative search, export, administrative, or similar permissions;
   e. any national, statewide, federal, or out-of-state sharing settings.

4. A copy of Flock Safety’s Settings & Configuration export for this agency, if available from the Flock Admin menu or any similar administrative/export function.

5. Records sufficient to show every Flock Safety, ALPR, or vehicle recognition camera owned, operated, accessed, funded, approved, or shared with this agency in or affecting Grayson, Georgia, the 30017 ZIP code, Bay Creek Precinct territory, or the surrounding unincorporated Gwinnett area. If exact coordinates are withheld, please provide the least-redacted existing record showing camera identifier, road/intersection, city or ZIP code, owner/operator, install date, and whether the camera is government-owned, HOA-owned, business-owned, school-owned, or privately shared.

6. Current policies, standard operating procedures, general orders, training materials, user guides, retention policies, hotlist policies, audit policies, misuse policies, and disciplinary policies governing Flock Safety, ALPR systems, vehicle recognition cameras, or camera-sharing systems.

7. Current contracts, purchase orders, invoices, quotes, statements of work, renewal documents, grants, memoranda of understanding, data-sharing agreements, intergovernmental agreements, letters of intent, and other agreement documents with Flock Safety, Flock Group, or any third party relating to Flock/ALPR services.

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine-readable format where available, including CSV, XLSX, PDF, native export, database export, or other spreadsheet format. Please do not redirect me to a public transparency portal in place of producing responsive records.

If any portion of a record is withheld, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and cite the specific Georgia Code section, subsection, and paragraph relied upon for each withholding or redaction.

Please notify me before incurring costs over $25. If any part of this request is considered too broad, please process items 1, 2, 3, and 4 first and contact me with a proposed narrowed scope for the remaining items.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Don Page

Thank you
